observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management undertake the following measures:
a. Instruct the GSO and the Accountable Officer to evaluate and determine the
current values of properties without costs and to identify unserviceable
properties for proper disposal thereof and subsequent derecognition from the
books of accounts;
b. Create Committee on Disposal and instruct them to fast track the disposal of
unserviceable properties using the most appropriate mode of disposition as
provided in Section 379 of RA 7160; and
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal
entry/ies to derecognize the unserviceable properties once these were actually
disposed.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a. Require the coordination between MA and GSO towards the
implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording supplies and
materials to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the
receipt and issuance of supplies and materials;
b. Require the GSO to prepare the SSMIs supported with RIS for supplies and
materials issued and the MA to prepare journal entry voucher supported by
SSMIs and RIS submitted by the GSO to record issuance of supplies and
materials; and
c. Instruct the MA and GSO to maintain SLC and stock cards, respectively,
and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate
corrections.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials in
charge of the distribution of relief goods to provide proof of distribution such as RDS to
be used by the Audit Team in validation, in compliance with Item VI of COA Circular
No. 2014-009.
We also recommend that the Accounting department use the appropriate account
in recording purchase of relief items intended for distribution or stockpiling for fair
presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to ensure compliance with
documentary requirements for every cash advance payroll; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to verify completeness of the required
supporting documents attached to the voucher before processing the
transactions and adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We commended the Management, particularly the Office of the Municipal
Accountant, the Property Supply Officer and the Inventory Team for their
commitment and efforts to comply with the conduct of annual physical inventory of all
its properties and submission of RPCPPE on time reconciled with the accounting
records.
However, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue a restitution of
Inventory Committee to update the name and designations of the employees in the new
Executive Order.

We commended the Management in the submission of the Report on Physical
Count of Semi-Expendable Property per office. However, we still recommended that
the Supply officer Designate properly maintain the RegSPI to monitor the location of
issued semi-expendable properties and prepare the RSPI, and furnish a copy to the
Accounting Office as supporting documentation for recording the issuance in the
agency’s books, in compliance with Items 4.7.4, Annex A.4, and 4.7.7, Annex A.7 of
COA Circular 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022.
Also, the Management require the Accounting Office to ensure that all journal
entries related to semi-expendable property issuances are duly supported by the RSPI
to maintain the accuracy and integrity of financial records and ensure that the Supply
Office strictly adheres to the guidelines on the issuance of semi-expendable properties
as required under COA Circular No. 2022-004.

We recommended that the management require the GSO to issue an inventory
custodian slip (ICS) to all properties and equipment which meet the definition and
criteria of a semi-expendable property, and costing below ₱50,000.00. Similarly, the
Accounting Office should initially recognize these purchases under the semi-
expendable property account in compliance with Section 4.1 of COA Circular 2022-
004 dated May 31, 2022.
Additionally, we recommended that the management strictly adhere to the
guidelines on the recognition, issuance to end –user and monitoring of semi-expendable
properties.

We recommended that Management submit a written justification as to the
cause of delay in the implementation and completion of 35 sanitary toilets, after the
fund was received in January, 2024. Also, the reason for non-preparation of the cost
per unit of the sanitary toilet by the Municipal Engineering Office to validate the total
cost incurred in the delivery of the project.
Additionally, submit to the Audit Team acknowledgement of the sanitary
toilets, duly received by the recipients/beneficiaries to support the actual turn-over of
35 units.
Finally, issue a memorandum to all department heads, informing them to
immediately coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for any received funds from
National Agencies for immediate use/implementation of the LGU.

We commended the Management for continuously extending assistance to the
National Government through faithful compliance of duties as withholding and
remitting agent of taxes in accordance with tax laws, rules and regulations.
However, we still recommended that the Accounting Department reconcile the
unremitted balance of withheld taxes from previous years and monitor closely the
preparation of the necessary Remittance Schedules.
Also, the Accounting Department maintain a Subsidiary Ledger for each
employee and supplier and the same be reconciled with the monthly remittances so that
any taxes withheld will be remitted in full and within the prescribed deadlines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the Human Resource Officer to reconcile
previous years’ records with the GSIS; and
b. MA and Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit all premium
contributions from prior years after reconciliation of records is made.

We commended Management’s compliance with existing laws, rules and
regulations on proper deduction and remittance of HDMF contributions and loan
amortization and PHIC premium contributions in CY 2024, in accordance with the
provisions of RA No. 9679 and RA No. 7875 as amended by RA Nos. 9241 and 10606.
However, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant and Human Resource Management Officer to reconcile prior
years’ records with the HDMF and PHIC. Also, the Municipal Treasurer to
immediately remit all premium contributions from prior years after reconciliation of
records is made.

We recommended that Management limit the payroll deductions to the order of
preference provided in the GAA and ensure that the approval of loan applications of
personnel should not cause their net take home pay to be below ₱5,000.00 as required
by the GAA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned municipal
officials:
a. Discontinue the payment of registration fees to various leagues and
associations for the conduct of regular and routinary meetings within the
Province;
b. Consider proposing to their respective leagues that the conduct of 1-day
meetings may be done through teleconferencing to eliminate the incurrence
of traveling expenses and registration fees, or holding them at government-
operated function halls, session halls and event centers to minimize costs; and
c. Exercise due prudence in the disbursement of government funds to avoid
incurrence of unnecessary expenditures.

We commended the Management for the substantial implementation of
development projects programmed for CY 2024 as provided under JMC No. 2020-1
dated November 4, 2020.
Nonetheless, we recommended that Management ensure strict adherence to all
work items specified in the contracts. Additionally, initiate the reporting of contractors
found to be violating the safety rules and regulations to the Philippine Contractors
Accreditation Board (PCAB) pursuant to Section 29 of the DOLE DO No. 198, series
of 2018.
Furthermore, we recommended that Management should strive for total
implementation of all projects embodied in the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Head of the Human
Resource Management Office to:
a. Formulate policies and procedures in recruitment and engagement of JO
personnel to strengthen the internal control pertaining thereto, and
subsequently submit a copy to the Audit Team;
b. Conduct personnel review, assessment and evaluation of the existing
manpower complement of the LGU to determine the necessity and propriety
of hiring JO personnel;
c. Apply the appropriate daily rate of Job Order in relation to the need and of
the agency and based on the educational attainment, technical expertise and
experience of the JOs, the rate should be clearly specified in the policy to be
developed by the HRMO; and
d. Strictly review the veracity of the documents attached to the payrolls
accomplishment reports and daily time records as well as ensure that all JO
contracts are signed and acknowledged by respective JO personnel.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
a. Human Resource Management Officer
 To submit to the AT a copy of perfected contracts within five (5)
working days after its execution in compliance with Section 3.1.1 of COA
Circular No. 2009-001; and
 To review the Job Order Contract to ensure that the JO workers have
duly signed and accepted the stipulations of the contract prior to
rendering service.
b. Municipal Accountant to ensure the existence of duly executed Job
Order contract signed by the JO worker prior to payment of wages.

We commended the Management for substantial implementation of LDRRM
projects in compliance with Rule 1, Sections 3 (e) and Rule 4, Section 4(1) of the
Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 10121.
Likewise, we recommended that Management should strive for total
implementation of all projects embodied in the 5% LDRRM Fund.

We recommended that the Management, through the GAD Focal Point System,
expedite the full establishment of the GAD database in order to have a basis for
planning and programming PPAs and formulating policies that are gender responsive.
Also, direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) or its TWG to provide technical
assistance to the MPDO in ensuring that the GAD Database is operational and updated.

We prospectively recommended that the LSB:
a. Furnish the Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer with SEF Budget within the prescribed timeline;
b. Submit and furnish a copy of the quarterly reports (Report of SEF
Utilization) to the Department of Education (DepEd), Department of Budget
and Management (DBM) and Department of Interior and Local
Government (DILG) within the prescribed periods; and
c. Direct the DepEd representative in the LSB to prepare the consolidated
reports.

We recommended that the LCE inform the Office of the Auditor within 10 days
after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity
of any postings of project signboards and/or public notice for validation by the AT.

We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC to:
a. Prepare and submit an approved PMR in accordance with the pertinent
provisions of RA No. 9184 and in the format prescribed in GPPB Circular
No. 03-2017; and
b. Submit to the GPPB the APP including the supplemental APP and revisions,
if any, as required by Section 7.3.5 of the RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Reconcile subsidiary records against the PPE ledger balances to ascertain the validity and accuracy of the account balances and ensure fair presentation thereof in the Financial Statements.
b. Maintain complete, updated and properly accomplished PPELCs for each class of PPE, listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description, custody, useful life and other necessary information; and
c. Undertake collaborative procedures with the GSO in the reconciliation of accounting and property records;

Likewise, we recommended that Management direct the GSO to:
a. Maintain complete, updated and properly accomplished Property Cards for each category of PPE listed in the accounting records and ensure that the balances shown thereon agree with the prepared RPCPPE and the book balances; and
b. Coordinate with the MAO in the reconciliation of property and accounting records.

a. Require an explanation from the heads of offices on the absence in the APP or the Supplemental APP of the specific appropriation for the purchase of airline tickets; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Reconcile subsidiary records against the PPE ledger balances to ascertain the validity and accuracy of the account balances and ensure fair presentation thereof in the Financial Statements.
Maintain complete, updated and properly accomplished PPELCs for each class of PPE, listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description, custody, useful life and other necessary information; and
Undertake collaborative procedures with the GSO in the reconciliation of accounting and property records;
Likewise, we recommended that Management direct the GSO to:
Maintain complete, updated and properly accomplished Property Cards for each category of PPE listed in the accounting records and ensure that the balances shown thereon agree with the prepared RPCPPE and the book balances; and
Coordinate with the MAO in the reconciliation of property and accounting records.
Management Comment/Action:
During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant stated that documents related to mentioned PPEs were not provided to their office to facilitate the proper recording of the PPEs.
Nonetheless, the Management concurred with the audit observation and signified compliance with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the MENRO to furnish the MAO and the GSO with the PTR to facilitate the recording of the PPEs and to ensure that the properties are properly stored for eventual use.
Prospectively, we also recommended that Management ensure that all relevant documents are obtained for proper accounting and recording of In-Kind Donations.
Management Comment/Action:
During the exit meeting, the Management concurred with the audit observation and signified compliance with the recommendations.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the preparation of   the RPTAR, its submission to the Municipal Treasurer and eventually to the Municipal Accountant as basis for RPT collection and set-up un the books of accounts as required by Section 248 of RA 7160; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to adopt the modified accrual method of accounting income from RPT to comply with Section 19 b of the NGAS Manual for LGUs and for fair representation of pertinent accounts presented in the FS per Section 27 of the IPSAS.
Management Comment/Action:
During the exit meeting, the Municipal Assessor asserted that they already submitted relevant reports to the Municipal Treasurer. However, according to the Municipal Accountant, no reports were forwarded to their office.
In the reply of the Acting Municipal Treasure to the AT, she stated that their Office submitted to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the certified list of all real property tax collectibles for the year 2024 and the summary of real property tax delinquency as of December 31, 2023 generated from Enhance Tax Revenue Assessment and Collection System (ETRACS), wherein receiving copy of the mentioned documents were attached.
Moreover, the Management signified their compliance with the recommendations.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Offices of the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer should follow the timelines in the submission of documents relevant to the setting up of RPT Receivable by the Municipal Accountant at the start of the year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert the balance of payables outstanding for more than 2 years from date of issuance which no actual claim has been filed, to the unappropriated surplus to conform with Section 98 of PD No. 1445 and to achieve faithful presentation of the account in the financial statements per Item 27 of IPSAS 1.
Management Comment/Action:
During the exit meeting, the Management concurred with the observation and signified compliance with the recommendation.

We recommended that management:
Coordinate with the barangay captain of Barangay District 2 to facilitate use of the Day Care Center, ensure that it is used as intended and guarantee its cleanliness and continuous maintenance;
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to inspect the DCC in Barangay District 3 and look into the possibility of requesting the contractor of its phase 1 to undertake necessary remedial measures, if warranted;
Direct the Inspection Committee to conduct thorough inspections of completed projects prior to final acceptance of the projects, in order to ensure that the projects have no defects arising during the warranty period; and
Require the Local Development Council to carefully and judiciously plan the development projects to be charged to the 20% DF to ensure that barangay projects charged thereto are used as intended to optimize LGU resources and for beneficiaries to attain maximum benefit therefrom.
Management Comment/Action:
In the letter reply of the Municipal Engineer (ME) to the AT, she stated that due to the insufficient fund allotted to the 20% Development Fund, it was agreed that every barangay must have at least one funded project from the said fund, even if by phase. They have ensured that upon turnover, the Punong Barangay must ensure the cleanliness and maintenance of the completed projects.
Regarding the District 3 Day Care Center, the ME mentioned that they have instructed the contractor to take remedial measures for the minor defects.
Moreover, during the exit meeting, the Management concurred with the observations and signified compliance with the recommendations.

We recommended that management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to continue to comply with the transfer of the unexpended balance to the STF as required;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transfer the corresponding cash of the CY 2023 unexpended balance from the GF to the Trust Fund-MDRRM.
Require the Municipal Accountant to resolve the difference of ₱1,851.19 in the Trust Fund books, prepare the necessary journal entries to reconcile the balances in the books of accounts and revert the amount of ₱7,138.99 to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.
Management Comment/Action:
The Municipal Accountant expressed difficulty to transfer the actual cash backup to the Trust Fund at year-end.
Moreover, the ₱1,851.19 difference pertains to a long outstanding guaranty deposit and an adjustment will be made by the MA to correct the difference.
The Management signified compliance with the recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the designated LDRR Officer prepare the LDRRMFIP annually and in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Budget Officer incorporate the same in the AIP so that the Annual Budget of the LGU would include programs, plans and activities of the LDRR not only for the current year but also the prior years’ unexpended balances; and
Require the LDRRM Officer and the Municipal Budget Officer to monitor the utilization of the LDRRMF so that completed PPAs with petty balances or funds for unimplemented PPAS which would not be carried out could be pooled or be made available respectively to finance new projects of the LDRRM under a revised AIP as necessary.
Management Comment/Action:
The LDRRMO replied that the LGU intends to accumulate the funds to undertake a more advantageous project.
Nonetheless, during the exit meeting, the management assured the AT that the above recommendations will be complied.

We recommended that the LCE:
Direct the MAO and the MSWDO to submit and explanation on the non-completion of the projects mentioned on the timelines set; and
Instruct the MSWDO and the MAO to expedite the utilization of the funds from the LGSF and the MSWDO, or return the same to the source agencies if implementation is not workable.
Management Comment/Action:
During the exit meeting, the MSWDO mentioned that they have devised new planned activities for the LGSF in 2024 and stated that as of April 2024, implementation is ongoing.
Moreover, the Management concurred with the observations and signified compliance with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Acting MENRO to conduct a review of the MOA entered into with the DENR-EMB V, identify the areas of concern and recommend action/s to ensure the LGU’s commitment to comply the salient provisions thereof; and
Enjoin the support of the Committee on Environment of the Sangguniang Bayan of Gigmoto for evaluation of the SWM Program of the LGU; the necessary infrastructure, manpower and the funds to ensure program continuity and implementation; otherwise, inform the source agency of the current status of the equipment for it to adopt measures to ensure that the equipment will be utilized as intended.
Management Comment/Action:
During the exit meeting, the Acting MENRO reiterated the mentioned circumstances upon delivery of the equipment; the LGU being unprepared of its storage area and that after determining the proposed facility to house the equipment, the DENR upon inspection recommended the latter would be used instead as the temporary residual containment area.
Nonetheless, the Management concurred with the audit observation and signified compliance with the recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Engineer to ensure submission of documents required by Section 9.1 of COA Circular 2012-001; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to  defer processing of claims for payment of infrastructure projects until the required supporting documents are attached to the claims and/or submitted to the AT for contract review.
Management Comment/Action:
During the exit meeting, the Management concurred with the audit observation and signified compliance with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Management direct the Bid and Awards Committee to invite observers from duly recognized private group with relevant knowledge on the procurement to be undertaken and ensure compliance with the requirements of Sections 13.1 and 13.2 of the RIRR of RA 9184.
Management Comment/Action:
In the letter reply of the BAC Chairman to the AT and during the exit meeting, they have concurred with the audit observation and signified compliance with the recommendation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that the validity of the Performance Security of the projects provided by the winning bidder extends up to the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance in accordance with Sections 39.1, 39.3, and 62.2.1 of Revised IRR of RA 9184;
Require the Municipal Engineer to monitor project accomplishments to provide a basis for timely extension of Performance Security up to the actual period of completion; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to validate the Performance Security attached to the claims for payment if its validity covers up to final acceptance of the project.
For projects not yet issued a Certificate of Final Inspection, management should require the winning bidder to extend the validity of the Performance Security covering the one-year period from date of completion.
Management Comment/Action:
During the exit meeting, the Management resolved that the contractors will be required to extend the validity of the performance security to 1.5 years from signing of the contract. If the performance security's expiration date precedes the issuance of the certificate of final inspection, contractors will be obliged to extend its validity.
With this, the Management concurred with the observation and signified compliance with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
Instruct the end-users to submit to the BAC the PRs for consolidation, evaluation and recommendation of the appropriate mode of procurement to be adopted; and
Require the BAC to prepare the BAC Resolution recommending the appropriate mode of procurement for the goods and services after evaluation in consonance with Section 12 of RA 9184 and the Resolution as support document to the claim for payment.
We also recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to defer processing of claims relative to procurement sans the approved BAC Resolution of the procurement mode adopted to ensure that the transactions had undergone BAC evaluation.
Management Comment/Action:
During the exit meeting, the Management concurred with the observation and signified compliance with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require an explanation from the heads of offices on the absence in the APP or the Supplemental APP of the specific appropriation for the purchase of airline tickets;
Direct all department heads/end users to include in their Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) all acquisitions of airline tickets planned for the year and submit the same to the BAC Secretariat for consolidation and basis for preparation of the APP;
Ensure that no procurement shall be authorized and undertaken unless it is in accordance with an approved APP or supplemental APP; and
Instruct all officials and employees to purchase airline tickets directly from commercial airlines and service providers as provided under Section V, Item b of Annex H of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
Management Comment/Action:
In the letter reply of the BAC Chairman to the AT, she stated that the department heads/end users were unaware of the need to include the procurement of airline tickets in the preparation of the APP.
Nevertheless, the Management committed to adhering to the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the BAC to explain the adoption of shopping as the mode of procurement for the transactions mentioned; and
Direct the BAC to conduct careful evaluation of all procurement transactions and recommend the appropriate mode of procurement to avoid possible suspensions or disallowances of the payments made.
Management Comment/Action:
In the letter of the BAC Chairman to the AT, the BAC assured that it would conduct a thorough evaluation of all procurements and recommend the appropriate mode of procurement to the concerned department heads/end-users.

We recommended that management should ensure that expenses for meals and snacks served during regular meetings and committee hearings, and other activities whose performance is basic to the LGU are not charged to the funds of the agency to avoid disallowance of unnecessary expenses as defined in Item 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012
We also recommended that in the preparation of the annual plans and programs of the agency, offices allocating for catering should indicate the specific activity to be funded and the cost it will entail for guidance and efficient use of funds.
Management Comment/Action:
In the letter reply of the Municipal Agriculturist to the AT, she claimed the agricultural activities mentioned in the AOM were indicated in the Annual Plans & Programs. She explained that the provision of meals and snacks was essential for the participants' basic needs, considering the distances they traveled for the trainings.
Moreover, during the exit meeting, the Accountant I clarified that some of the disbursements/programs enumerated in the AOM, such as those pertains to BFP and PNP, were included in the POPS Plan but the sponsoring agency was not the LGU. However, the LGU was unable to document clearly the responsibilities of the respective agencies.
Nevertheless, the Management concurred with audit observation and signified compliance with the recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Require the contractor to refund the amount of ₱13,584.92 equivalent to the excess of the contract cost over  the reasonable computation (recomputed ABC) using DO 197 as basis; and
Direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure the reasonableness of the computation of the ABC for all infrastructure projects by adhering to the provisions of applicable laws, rules and regulations to avoid excessive computation of the ABC.
Management Comment/Action:
In the letter reply of the Municipal Engineer to the AT, the contractors have refunded the ₱13,584.92 excess of the contract cost over the reasonable computation. Additionally, they have confirmed their adherence with the computation of ABC as stated in DPWH DO No. 197.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive expedite the hiring of new employees and fill-up the vacant position in the LGU funded under the CY 2023 Annual Budget in accordance with the Civil Service Commission rules and regulations so that it could improve its services and programs to the public and provide job opportunities to the community.
Management Comment/Action:
During the exit meeting, the Municipal Mayor stated that some of the positions were already filled up in the 1st quarter of CY 2024.
The Management signified compliance with the recommendation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the General Services Officer to submit promptly to COA copies of contracts, purchase orders and notices of deliveries pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 96-010.
Management Comment/Action:
During the exit meeting, the Management concurred with the audit observation and signified compliance with the recommendation.

We recommended that the agency strictly ensure that disbursements for Registration/Investment Fees are duly approved and authorized in the Annual Budget or Supplemental Budget as necessary to avoid the incurrence of unauthorized disbursements.
It is further recommended that transactions are recorded using the appropriate accounts.
Management Comment/Action:
During the exit meeting, the Management concurred with the audit observation and signified compliance with the recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Require the legislative branch to prepare an activity plan for the participation of its members to league seminars and activities to maximize resources and establish prioritization of attendance;
Encourage participation to seminars/conventions conducted within the province/region when these are conducted within the geographical area to lessen costs;
Direct municipal officials to ensure that payment of registration fees in attending seminars, trainings, assemblies and conferences sponsored by their respective leagues are supported with proper government official receipts; and
Exercise due prudence in the disbursement of government funds to avoid incurrence of excessive expenditures.
Management Comment/Action:
During the exit meeting, the Management have concurred with the observation and signified compliance with the recommendations.

We recommended that Management direct the personnel in-charge of check preparation to exercise prudence in the preparation of checks to minimize or avoid wastage of accountable forms.
Management Comment/Action:
During the exit meeting, the Management concurred with the audit observation and signified compliance with recommendation.

Conduct reconciliation of the accounts with the journals, ledgers and source documents.  Afterwards, make representation with the concerned government agencies with regards to the discrepancies noted in the books of the LGU, and adjust accordingly;
After reconciliation, remit immediately the determined unpaid obligations to the concerned government agencies to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges for delay or non-remittance; and
Henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation of account balances.

We recommended that the incumbent Acting Municipal Treasurer ensure that she is properly bonded and initiate renewal of bond before or 30 days prior to the expiration of her current bond.

direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the balance of the Cash – Local Treasury account in the General Fund and the SEF to conform with Section 111 of PD 1445; and

require the Municipal Treasurer effect the adjustments to the Cash - Local Treasury account both in the GF and the SEF cash books.

We also recommended continued compliance with Section 74 of PD 1445 and Section 3.2 of COA Circular 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that the agency head:

direct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct an inventory of unreleased checks to ensure that the long outstanding checks were truly issued to the payees; if checks were still in custody of the MTO, payees should be informed of their unclaimed checks for replacement if warranted;

require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a logbook for checks issued with payees signing thereon upon receipt; and

direct the Municipal Accounting Office to reclassify unclaimed stale checks to Accounts Payable to adjust both the payables and the Cash in Bank account balances.
observations.managementAction: A log book of released checks was maintained at the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the MT submit the monthly CRAAF accompanied by the RAAF per AO to the Audit Team, through the Accounting Office not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month as provided under the NGAS Manual.

require the MHO to render a report to the Municipal Accountant on the receipt, issuance, and balances of vaccines directly received from outside sources; and 

require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MHO, to determine the full cost of ending inventory of vaccines as at year end, if any and henceforth recognize and record future receipts and issuances of the same.

instruct the Municipal Agriculture Officer to conduct a review and evaluation of the projects originally funded by the LGSF received in 2016 and submit project status report thereof;

require the Local Finance Committee to prepare quarterly reports on fund utilization and status of program/project and post the same to the LGUs website or in any 3 conspicuous places in the municipality to comply with Item 5 of LBC 109; and

advise the Local Chief Executive (LCE) on the Status of Funds and the nature of expenses charged therefrom.

We also recommended that such enhanced guidelines shall cover all LGU JO applicants, both from executive and legislative departments.
observations.managementAction: Guidelines for TRABAHO PARA SA ESTUDYANTE PROGRAM was adopted by the LGU in the hiring of Job Order workers.

observations.recommendation:

to prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers to comply with Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012;

in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, exert extra efforts to work back on the transactions charged to the LDRRMF and update the subsidiary ledgers of trust funds per year of transfer; and

to disclose in the notes to financial statements the detailed unexpended balance of the LDRRMF as of year-end using the format prescribed by Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the preparation and submission of the necessary documents required by the bank to reactivate the bank account for PRDP. After such, the LGU shall return immediately the unused balance of PRDP Funds to the DA PSO to comply with Section 4.1.5.8 of PRDP FMOG.

We recommended the following:

the LCE to direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the submission of the documentary requirements needed by the bank in order to reactivate the bank account of PRDP. After such, the LCE cause the immediate settlement of the outstanding obligations of PRDP to the General Fund and refund the retention money to the concerned suppliers; and

the LGU to return the initial deposit advanced by the GF and initiate the closing of the bank accounts to avoid unnecessary bank charges.
observations.managementAction: PRDP bank account was reactivated and closed on April 13, 2023. The remaining local fund balance was transferred to GF. The unexpended balance of the GOP & grant was returned to PRDP Regional Office on April 28, 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

explain the advance payment of YEB and Cash gift for CY 2022;

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require:
The MDRRM Officer to prepare the necessary SSMIs supported with RIS for the food supplies already issued, particularly those pertaining to previous years’ purchases;
The MA to prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) as supported by SSMIs and RIS submitted by the MDRRM Officer to adjust the Inventory account that still exist per accounting records but already issued and inexistent in the bodega/MDRRM Office; and
The MA to demand from concerned officials/personnel who requested for inventories for distribution to various beneficiaries to submit immediately without fail the RIS, Acknowledgement Receipts and other related documents relative to the food supplies issued to them for distribution.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to:
prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis; and
perform constant review of recorded transactions to deter error and omissions.
We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC to ensure compliance with posting requirements for procurement under emergency cases pursuant to Section 54.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the MA and the AMT conduct the reconciliation at least once every quarter for early detection of errors and to prepare appropriate adjustments to bring the records into agreement and to avoid shortage/overage of reported amount in the cashbook and the general ledger.

We recommended that Management require the AOs strictly observe rules and regulations governing proper handling of collections pursuant to the aforementioned law and regulations.  Ensure that all collections for the day are turned over to the AMT and deposited intact at least once a week to the government authorized depository bank or as soon as it reaches ₱10,000.00.

We recommended that the Management require ICFP team personnel to implement immediately all the activities contained in the approved ICFP Project to fully maximize the benefits therefrom by intended beneficiaries.  Likewise, we recommended that the MNAO to secure the MOA to determine the conditions and terms of implementation of the BUB projects.

We recommended that the LCE direct the AMT to prioritize and fast track the submission of the documentary requirements needed by the bank in order to reactivate the bank account for PRDP. After such, the LGU shall return immediately to the DA PSO the unused balance of PRDP Funds in accordance with Section 4.1.5.8 of PRDP FMOG and secure an official/ acknowledgment receipt accordingly.

We recommended that the LCE direct the AMT to prioritize the submission of the documentary requirements needed by the bank in order to reactivate the bank account of PRDP. After such, the LCE cause the immediate settlement of the outstanding obligations of PRDP. The LGU shall also determine whether they will undertake another project under the PRDP. If otherwise, the LGU shall return the initial deposit advanced by the GF and initiate the closing of the bank accounts to avoid unnecessary bank charges.
We also recommended that the LCE immediately refund the retention money to the concerned suppliers.

We recommended that the LCE strictly enforce compliance with the requirements set forth in the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001. Further, require the MA to record the grant of financial assistance to NGOs under the Due from NGOs/POs Account and require the liquidation thereof.

We recommended that Management monitor and ensure the implementation and completion of all the LGUs programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% DF to immediately serve the intended purpose or use thereof to the constituents.

We commended the LCE and his officials for a job well done.  Nevertheless, we recommended Management to continue the good work and monitor closely the implementation of all DRRMF PPAs to attain 100 per cent accomplishments.

We recommended that Management:
Provide a stock room for proper storage of items of inventories and require the coordination between the MA and the Supply Officer (SO) designate towards the implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording of supplies and materials;
Require the SO designate to prepare the SSMIs supported with RIS for supplies and materials issued and the MA to prepare JEV as supported by SSMIs and RIS submitted by the SO to record issuance of supplies and materials; and
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of supplies and materials every semester and submit the Physical Inventory Report to the Auditor’s Office as required in Section 124, Volume 1 of the MNGAs.

We recommended that the LCE require:
The MDRRM Officer to prepare SSMI together with the RIS and liquidation reports/relief distribution sheets and submit same to the MA for recording;
The MDRRM Officer to conduct immediately the inventory of the remaining undistributed food supplies. Henceforth, cause the conduct of physical inventory of the food supplies every semester, the report on which shall be submitted to the Auditor, on or before July 31 and January 31 after the end of each semester, for verification; and
The MDRRM Officer and the MA to reconcile their records based on the actual physical count of inventories.

We recommended that the MA ensure full disclosure of information in the Notes to FS as required under COA Circular No. 2012-02 to enhance the relevance and understandability of the FS to the users of the same.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to prepare and the DRRM Officer to submit without fail the report on the sources and utilization of the LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that the BAC observes the disclosure of the ABC in the RFQ in accordance with the provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 in order to obtain the most advantageous price for its procurement transactions.

We recommended that the LCE ensure that all transactions pertaining to procurement should be evaluated by the BAC, and a corresponding resolution thereto on the mode of procurement to be adopted shall be prepared for approval of the LCE. On the acquisition of food items for the SFP, the Management may consider segregating the non-perishable and perishable items during the preparation of work and financial plan. The non-perishable items shall be acquired thru public bidding or any alternative mode of procurement (with emphasis on negotiated procurement through community participation) in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184 and terms and conditions of the MOA. On the other hand, perishable goods and other items where application of procurement laws under RA 9184 may not be feasible and for justifiable reasons may be acquired by way of special purpose cash advances following usual budget, accounting and auditing rules and DSWD AO No. 4 Series of 2016 .
We also recommended that the LCE ensure compliance with the MOA for all fund transfers received from the DSWD, as well as all National Government Agencies. All procurements shall be in accordance with procurement laws, rules and regulations and budgeting, accounting and auditing rules and regulations, as applicable.

We recommended for the LCE not to allow the payment of travel expenses to job-order personnel. Stop the practice of allowing these Job Order workers to attend conferences, trainings and other travel related tasks. We further recommended that in cases when job-order personnel are directed for a task that requires travel, travel expenses shall be charged to the person authorizing the travel, or allow them  to claim only the actual fare from their station to a particular place and vice versa.

We recommended that the AMT submit the monthly CRAAF accompanied by the RAAF per AO to the Audit Team, through the Accounting Office not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month as provided under the NGAS manual.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant (MA) to reconcile the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 to correct the balances of said accounts.

c. Require the designated Property Officer to maintain property cards and update them periodically to aid in the conduct of physical count and to solve or minimize the perennial problem of unreconciled Accounting and Property records.
d. Likewise, the Committee should be warned that failure to perform their duties as Chairman and Members of the Inventory Committee may be a valid ground for filing of administrative charges for gross neglect of duty.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all concerned personnel to comply with the preparation of records/reports prescribed under COA Circular 2015-008.

We recommended that the LCE direct all employees with unliquidated cash advances to liquidate immediately cash advances for official travels in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 97-002.  See to it that all cash advances are liquidated at year-end.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to review immediately the balances of the trust accounts to determine the LGU’s actual liability to the concerned government agencies, and prepare the appropriate adjustments.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit strictly all collections intact, consistent with the provisions of Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to effect the immediate transfer of the unexpended cash balances of DRRMF-QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the special trust fund.  Likewise, the Municipal Accountant shall ensure proper and complete recording of all intra-agency receivables and payables relative to DRRMF transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Treasurer to provide the LSB with the correct amount of funds available for appropriations.  Also, we recommended that the LSB convene and prepare a comprehensive annual supplemental budget for the operation and maintenance of public schools within the municipality with specific appropriations giving priority to the: 1.) construction, repair, and maintenance of school buildings and other facilities; 2.) establishment and maintenance of extension classes; and 3.) sports activities.
We also recommended that the LSB observe strictly the provision of Sections 99 & 100 of RA 7160 and to adhere strictly on  the timelines in the approval of budget.

We recommended that the LCE, being the Head of the LSB to direct the members of the LSB, the Municipal General Services Officer and the BAC to observe that procurement of goods should be done through public bidding.  Other modes of procurement as provided under R.A. 9184 could be resorted to if public bidding is not applicable.  All transactions pertaining to procurement should be evaluated by the BAC and a corresponding resolution thereto should be prepared and attached to the disbursement voucher in consonance with the provision of Section 12 of RA 9184.
Also, we recommended that the LCE require the Accountant not to process disbursement vouchers relative to procurement transactions without the necessary documents and approved BAC resolutions to ensure that all transactions have undergone BAC evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipality comply immediately with the information and documents required by the EMB Regional Office No. V without prejudice to the immediate closure and rehabilitation of its existing open dumpsite as directed by the same office per its letter dated February 3, 2020.  Moreover, prepare the proposed sanitary landfill to accommodate solid wastes upon the imminent closure of the dumpsite.  Such immediate action is necessary to address if not prevent, detrimental ecological issues and their effects on human health and to the environment.
The LGU should prepare a comprehensive work plan for SWM operation indicating all activities needed to perform efficiently and effectively SWM functions with corresponding budget per activity.  This would more or less guide the LGU in the implementation and in assessing the SWM operations.
In addition, institutionalize the Solid Waste Management program by creating a separate office with separate budget and with permanent appointed personnel as Head of Office to run its affairs.

We recommended that the LCE:
Secure a report of the Joint Inspection Team with satisfactory remark and acceptance of completed subproject by the RPCO, and thereafter, issue a Certificate of Turn Over to the PG; and
After the completion of documentations for property turn-over, prepare the necessary journal entries to close the project equity/ trust liability accounts.

We recommended that the LCE fast track the implementation of projects that should have been completed in the first semester.  Also, require the MPDO to submit monitoring report on its status of implementation, and use the same as basis in making decision in project implementations in accordance with the approved timetable/schedule embodied in the Annual Budget.
Likewise, the implementation of CY 2019 20% DF projects must be fast tracked so that benefits therefrom could be realized at the earliest possible time.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to:
Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections; and
Prepare and submit regularly a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the Sanggunian for the body to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the Implementing Departments to confirm whether the projects charged to particular trust funds were actually completed and determine whether there are other outstanding obligations chargeable to the funds.  If projects were already completed and no other outstanding obligations accrue to the funds, return immediately the unexpended balance to SA. If the latter cannot be identified, remit immediately the remaining idle and unutilized funds to the BTr.
For the prior year unreconciled balances, the Municipal Accountant shall continue to conduct reconciliation and effect the necessary adjustments in the financial statements

We recommended that the LCE require the LDRRM Officer and other concerned agency officials to be decisive and focused in project implementation. Also, monitor and evaluate regularly the implementation of PPAs in compliance with Rule 4, Section 1 of the IRR of RA 10121 to ensure that preparedness, mitigation and rehabilitation projects are implemented in the corresponding budget year to avoid, lessen or limit the potential adverse impact of disasters.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to review immediately the loan amortization payments and other pertinent records to determine the LGU’s actual liability to the DOF-MDFO, and reconcile the same with their records, and prepare the appropriate adjustments.  This is without prejudice to the penalty imposed for late payment which should be shouldered by liable persons. (See Observation No. 16)
Specifically, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer should coordinate with the focal person of the DOF-MDFO and request for reconciliation of their transactions and records so that appropriate adjustments could be effected.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with Department of Finance, Municipal Development Fund Office to request for reconsideration for the penalty imposed for late payment and apply the same to the principal component of the loan.  Otherwise, the LCE shall require the Municipal Treasurer to shoulder personally the penalty for late payment of the loan amortization amounting to ₱96,510.
Accordingly, a Notice of Disallowance covering the penalty will be issued if the request shall not be approved favorably.

We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC that procurement of fuel, oil and lubricants shall be done by way of public bidding or any alternative mode of procurement as provided under RA 9184 and as prescribed under GPPB Circular No. 5-2018.   All transactions pertaining to procurement should be evaluated by the BAC, and a corresponding resolution thereto should be prepared and attached to the corresponding DV.  Moreover, the procedures, controls, forms and reports prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61 shall be strictly complied with.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant:
Not to process disbursement vouchers relative to fuel, oil and lubricant transactions without the necessary documents evidencing the conduct of proper procurement process as provided under RA 9184 and the forms and reports required by COA Circular 77-61; and
Record said transactions using the perpetual inventory method in accordance with the NGAS manual

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and all departments involved in the preparations of financial reports to observe Sections 2 & 4 of COA Circular 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010 and the preparation of monthly and quarterly financial reports as prescribed under Section 100 of PD 1445.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the supporting schedules of all accounts to the Auditor to establish the balances of the accounts.
